zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Waller於1495年出生在英國素福爾可沃薩姆時，她的父親Sir John Waller33歲，母親Margaret Thorolde29歲。她於1512年與Thomas Sherman在英國英格蘭素福爾可亞可利結婚。他們至少是11個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1573年3月1日在英國素福爾可亞可利享年78歲，埋葬在英國素福爾可亞可利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Waller于1495年出生在英国素福爾可沃薩姆时，她的父亲Sir John Waller33岁，母亲Margaret Thorolde29岁。她于1512年与Thomas Sherman在英国英格兰素福爾可亞可利结婚。他们至少是11个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1573年3月1日在英国素福爾可亞可利享年78岁，埋葬在英国素福爾可亞可利。

nameMeanings.name: Waller
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English waler, waliere, walour, waller ‘builder of walls, mason’.
English: in Sussex, perhaps a topographic name for someone who lived by a prominent wall.

English: topographic name for someone who lived by a spring, stream, or man-made well, from Middle English waller, a derivative of Middle English walle, Old English wælle, wælle. It is a West Midlands dialect form of Weller .

variants.name: Weller
meanings: English (Surrey, Sussex, and Kent): topographic name for someone who lived by a spring, stream, or man-made well, from Middle English weller, willer, an agent derivative of Middle English welle (in southern England) and wille (in south western England), from Old English wella, well(e), wiella, wielle, willa, wyll(a), wylle. This type of name, a topographic term + -er, is particularly typical of Sussex and its neigbouring counties.
English: occupational name for someone who casts metal or who boils salt, from Middle English weller, willer, a derivative with -er of Old English wellan, wiellan ‘to boil’. Compare 5 below.
North German: variant of   Welle  , cognate with 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Waller was born in 1495, in Wortham, Suffolk, England, her father, Sir John Waller, was 33 and her mother, Margaret Thorolde, was 29. She married Thomas Sherman in 1512, in Yaxley, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 11 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 1 March 1573, in Yaxley, Suffolk, England, at the age of 78, and was buried in Yaxley, Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Waller est née en 1495, à Wortham, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Waller, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Margaret Thorolde, avait 29 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Sherman en 1512, à Yaxley, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 11 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 1 mars 1573, à Yaxley, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 78 ans, et a été enterrée à Yaxley, Suffolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Waller 1495 in Wortham, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John Waller 33 und ihre Mutter Margaret Thorolde 29. Sie hat 1512 in Yaxley, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom Thomas Sherman geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 11 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 1. März 1573 im Alter von 78 Jahren in Yaxley, Suffolk, England und wurde in Yaxley, Suffolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Waller è nata il nel 1495 nel Wortham, Suffolk, England, sua padre, Sir John Waller, aveva 33 anni e sua madre, Margaret Thorolde, aveva 29 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Sherman nel 1512, nel Yaxley, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 11 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 1 marzo 1573, nel Yaxley, Suffolk, England, all'età di 78 anni, e fu sepolta a Yaxley, Suffolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Wallerが1495年にイギリススフォルクウオルサムで生まれた時，父Sir John Wallerは33歳，母Margaret Thoroldeは29歳であった。1512年にイギリスイギリススフォルクヤクスリーでThomas Shermanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも11人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1573年3月1日にイギリススフォルクヤクスリーで78歳で死去し，イギリススフォルクヤクスリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Waller(이)가 1495년 영국 수폴크 워섬에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Waller(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Thorolde(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 1512년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 야스리에서 Thomas Sherman(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명 이상의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1573년 3월 1일 영국 수폴크 야스리에서 78세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수폴크 야스리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Waller nasceu em 1495, em Wortham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John Waller, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Thorolde, tinha 29 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Sherman em 1512, em Yaxley, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 11 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1 de março de 1573, em Yaxley, Suffolk, Inglaterra, com 78 anos, e foi sepultada em Yaxley, Suffolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Waller родилась в 1495 г. в местности вортам, суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John Waller было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret Thorolde, 29. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Sherman в 1512 г. в местности яксли, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 11 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла 1 марта 1573 г. в местности яксли, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 78 лет и была похоронена в местности яксли, суффолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Waller nació en 1495, en Wortham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John Waller, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Margaret Thorolde, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Thomas Sherman en 1512, en Yaxley, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 11 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 1 de marzo de 1573, en Yaxley, Suffolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 78 años, y fue sepultada en Yaxley, Suffolk, Inglaterra.
